payload.title: Site stabilization should detect legacy/current task substrate splits
payload.summary: While processing the Windows User Site inbox, current Narada CLI correctly wrote canonical task state to .ai/do-not-open/tasks, .ai/task-lifecycle.db, mutation evidence, and lifecycle snapshot, but the Site still had older .ai/tasks plus .ai/tasks/task-lifecycle.db recorded as durable history. The result looked like incoherent repository dirt and duplicate task authority until manually dearbitrized in Site config/docs/.gitignore. Narada should provide a governed Site stabilization/reconciliation command that detects this split and offers an explicit migration, archive, or compatibility posture.
evidence: User Site had legacy durable .ai/tasks/ with tasks 001-034 and tracked .ai/tasks/task-lifecycle.db.
Canonical inbox promotion created .ai/do-not-open/tasks/20260429-1-... plus .ai/task-lifecycle.db and .ai/task-lifecycle-snapshot.json.
Manual local fix had to update config durable/volatile paths, .gitignore, README, and KB decisions to explain ignored runtime SQLite versus Git-visible task evidence.
proposal: Add a Site stabilization/reconciliation surface that inspects task substrates, lifecycle DB locations, inbox DB posture, mutation evidence, snapshots, and legacy task directories.
The surface should emit a dearbitrized plan and execute migration, archive, or compatibility decisions with mutation evidence when authorized.
payload.recommendation: Treat this as execution work adjacent to Site stabilization and task lifecycle DB posture, not merely documentation.
